examples.title: CKY Algorithm
examples.description: Parsing with Chomsky Normal Form grammar
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CKY
INPUT: words w1...wn, CNF grammar G
INIT: table[1..n][1..n]
FOR i=1 TO n:
  table[i][i] = {A | A → wi ∈ G}
FOR l=2 TO n:
  FOR i=1 TO n-l+1:
    j = i + l -1
    FOR k=i TO j-1:
      table[i][j] += {A | A → BC ∈ G, B ∈ table[i][k], C ∈ table[k+1][j]}
RETURN table[1][n] contains S
examples.explanation: Fills DP table with possible non-terminals for substrings
